title: Modifying Content ✍️
description: Magically rewrite HTML and colors live without reloading.

slides.title: You Have The Power 🔋
slides.content:
# Live Editing

Once you have selected an HTML element and stored it in a variable, you have total god-like power over it.

You can alter its internal text, completely rewrite its HTML, or even overhaul its CSS styles on the fly without the user ever reloading the page!

slides.title: Changing Text 📝
Try It Out 💻
slides.content: Notice how we overwrite the text, and instantly the UI updates to show the new data without any reloading required!

slides.title: Changing HTML 🧱
slides.content:
## innerHTML What if you don't just want to change words, but actually want to inject bold tags `` or link tags `` directly inside the element? You cannot use `.textContent`. It is "safe" and will treat `` as literal dumb text. You must overwrite the **`.innerHTML`** property! ```html

I am a user.

``` ```javascript let bio = document.querySelector("#bio"); // The browser will ACTUALLY render this as bold HTML! bio.innerHTML = "I am a Pro user."; ```

quizData.question: What happens if you use `.textContent = "Hello"` on a paragraph tag?

options.text: It displays exactly what you typed, brackets and all: Hello

quizData.explanation: textContent strips away all HTML powers. It forces the browser to display exactly the dumb characters you provided. To render the bolding effect, you MUST use innerHTML.

slides.title: Changing Styles 🎨

quizData.question: How do you translate the CSS property 'margin-top' into JavaScript Dot Notation syntax?
quizData.explanation: Because hyphens (-) are read as 'subtraction signs' in JavaScript math, all CSS properties are converted to CamelCase (marginTop, fontStyle, zIndex) to avoid crashing the language.
